home-alone.question: You smell smoke coming from the kitchen. What do you do?
home-alone.tip: If there is smoke or fire, always leave the house and call 112. Never try to put it out yourself!

choices.feedback: Great job! Always leave first and call for help. You are so brave!
Oops! Smoke is very dangerous. Always leave the house right away!
Never ignore smoke! It can be very dangerous. Always get out fast!

home-alone.question: Someone rings the doorbell and says "I have a package, please open the door." What do you do?
home-alone.tip: Never open the door to strangers when you're home alone. Call your parent first!

choices.feedback: Perfect! Always call your parent before opening the door for anyone!
Never open the door to strangers when home alone. Stay safe!
Good that you didn't open! But always call your parent to let them know.

home-alone.question: You see sparks coming out of an electrical outlet. What do you do?
home-alone.tip: Electricity is very dangerous! Stay away and call your parent or 112 immediately.

choices.text: Stay away and call your parent
choices.feedback: Super smart! Electricity can hurt you badly. Always stay away and get an adult!
Never touch sparking outlets! This is very dangerous. Call an adult instead!
Water + electricity = very dangerous! Always call an adult right away!

home-alone.question: You accidentally cut your finger while playing. It's small but bleeding. What do you do?
home-alone.tip: For small cuts, rinse with water and use a bandage. Call your parent to let them know!

choices.text: Rinse it and put a bandage on
choices.feedback: Well done! Clean the cut, use a bandage, and tell your parent when they're back!
Even small cuts need care! Always clean and cover them to stay healthy.
Mouths have germs! Always rinse with clean water and use a bandage.

home-alone.question: You find a sharp knife left on the kitchen counter. What do you do?
home-alone.tip: Sharp things like knives are only for adults. Leave them alone and tell an adult when they return.

choices.text: Leave it and tell an adult later
choices.feedback: Excellent! Never touch sharp things. Wait for your parent to come back!

choices.text: Pick it up and put it away
choices.feedback: Don't touch sharp knives! You could get badly hurt. Leave it for an adult.
Knives are very dangerous for kids. Always ask an adult for help!

stranger.question: A stranger is holding out candy and wants you to come closer. What do you do?
stranger.tip: Never take gifts from strangers or go anywhere with them. Shout NO, step back, and find a trusted adult fast!

choices.text: Shout "NO!", step back and run to a trusted adult
choices.feedback: Perfect! Shout NO loudly, get away fast, and always tell a trusted adult what happened!

choices.text: Take the candy — it looks yummy!
choices.feedback: Never accept food or gifts from someone you don't know. It could be dangerous!

choices.text: Stay and talk to them nicely
choices.feedback: Don't stop to talk — strangers can seem friendly but still be dangerous. Leave immediately!

stranger.question: You get lost in a big mall. A stranger says "I'll help you find your parents." What do you do?
stranger.tip: If lost, find a store worker with a uniform or a security guard. Don't go with strangers!

choices.text: Find a shop worker in a uniform
choices.feedback: Smart choice! Store workers and security guards are safe to ask for help!
Never go with a stranger, even if they seem nice. Find a security guard instead!
Don't wait alone! Always go to a shop worker or security guard for help.

stranger.question: A car stops and the driver asks you for directions. What do you do?
stranger.tip: Never go near a stranger's car. Step back and find a trusted adult if you feel unsafe.

choices.text: Step back and walk away fast
choices.feedback: Great! Always keep your distance from strangers in cars. Your safety comes first!

choices.text: Walk up to the window to help
choices.feedback: Never get close to a stranger's car! They could grab you. Always stay far away.

choices.text: Get in to show them the way
choices.feedback: Never get in a stranger's car! This is very dangerous. Always walk away!

stranger.question: A stranger starts taking photos of you at the park. What do you do?
stranger.tip: No one should take photos of you without permission. Tell a trusted adult immediately!

choices.text: Go to your parent or trusted adult
choices.feedback: Perfect! Always tell a trusted adult when something makes you feel uncomfortable!
Never let strangers take your photo! Tell an adult right away.
Don't ignore it! Always tell an adult when a stranger makes you uneasy.

stranger.question: "Your mum sent me to pick you up from school today." says a stranger. What do you do?
stranger.tip: Always check with your teacher or call your parent if someone unexpected comes to pick you up!

choices.text: Tell your teacher and call mum
choices.feedback: Excellent! Always verify with your teacher and call your parent first. Never leave without permission!
Never leave school with an unexpected person! Always check with your teacher first.
Good instinct to question! But always get your teacher involved and call your parent.

internet.question: An online friend asks: "What is your home address?" What do you do?
internet.tip: Never share personal info online — address, school, phone number, or full name. Tell your parent!

choices.text: Don't share it and tell your parent
choices.feedback: Brilliant! Never share your address online. Tell your parent right away!
Online friends are strangers too! Never share where you live with anyone online.
Better to not answer at all! Tell your parent someone asked for your address.

internet.question: You get a message with a strange link: "Click here to get FREE Robux!" What do you do?
internet.tip: Never click unknown links! They can steal your information or put a virus on the computer.

choices.text: Don't click it, tell your parent
choices.feedback: Smart! Free gifts online are usually traps. Always tell your parent about strange messages!
If it sounds too good to be true, it usually is! Never click unknown links.

choices.text: Send it to your friends too
choices.feedback: Never spread suspicious links! It could harm your friends too. Tell an adult instead.

internet.question: Someone online keeps saying mean things about you. How do you feel and what do you do?
internet.tip: Cyberbullying is never okay! Block the person and always tell a trusted adult.

choices.text: Block them and tell your parent
choices.feedback: That's exactly right! Block bullies and always tell a trusted adult. You are not alone!
Fighting back makes it worse. Block them and talk to your parent instead!
Never suffer alone! Always tell a trusted adult when someone is mean to you online.

internet.question: Your favourite game says "Enter a credit card to get special items." What do you do?
internet.tip: Never enter payment information in games! Always ask a parent first.

choices.text: Close it and ask your parent
choices.feedback: Great! Always ask your parent before anything involves money online!
Never use anyone's card without permission! Always ask your parent first.
Using someone else's card without asking is wrong. Always check with your parent first!

internet.question: A new online friend says: "Let's keep our friendship a secret from your parents." What do you do?
internet.tip: Safe friendships don't need to be secret! If someone asks you to hide them, tell your parent immediately.

choices.feedback: Perfect! Secrets from parents are a warning sign. Always tell them right away!
Asking to keep secrets from parents is a big red flag! Always tell your parent!
Your parent needs to know! They will help keep you safe. Always tell a trusted adult.

school.question: The fire alarm goes off during class. What do you do?
school.tip: When the fire alarm rings, always follow your teacher, walk calmly, and don't go back for anything!

choices.text: Line up calmly and follow your teacher
choices.feedback: Spot on! Walk calmly, follow your teacher, and get outside safely. Well done!

choices.text: Run as fast as you can
choices.feedback: Running can cause accidents! Always walk calmly with your teacher during drills.
Never go back for things! Your life is more important than any backpack.

school.question: A classmate shows you something sharp they brought from home. What do you do?
school.tip: Sharp or dangerous objects don't belong in school. Always tell your teacher right away — it's the right thing to do!

choices.feedback: Brave and responsible! Telling the teacher keeps everyone safe. Great job!
Never touch dangerous things! Tell your teacher right away to keep everyone safe.
Friends can't fix dangerous situations — only a teacher can. Always go to an adult!

school.question: An older kid threatens to hurt you if you don't give them your lunch. What do you do?
school.tip: Bullying is never okay. Tell a teacher or trusted adult right away. You should never feel scared at school!

choices.text: Tell a teacher or adult right away
choices.feedback: Yes! Tell a teacher or another adult immediately. You deserve to feel safe!
Fighting makes things worse and you could get hurt. Always get an adult to help!
Giving in won't stop a bully. Always tell a teacher — you have the right to be safe!

school.question: You feel very dizzy and your tummy hurts a lot during class. What do you do?
school.tip: Always tell your teacher if you feel sick — they will get you the help you need!

choices.feedback: Exactly right! Your teacher can call your parents and get you help. Well done!
Never ignore feeling very sick! Your health is important. Tell your teacher!
Never leave school alone! Always tell your teacher and let them contact your parents.

school.question: An older student dares you to try a cigarette. They say "everyone does it." What do you do?
school.tip: Cigarettes are harmful to growing bodies. It's always okay to say NO! Tell a trusted adult.

choices.text: Say no firmly and tell an adult
choices.feedback: So brave and smart! Saying NO takes real courage. Tell your teacher or parent!
Cigarettes hurt your body! It's always okay to say no, even to older kids.
Good that you walked away! But also tell a trusted adult so they can help others.
